investigated their accuracy in code generation [14], code quality [23], impact on security [19], and their
potential for API learning and code complexity prediction. How this can be applied to game and engine de-
velopmentisanimportantandinterestingproblem.Challengesarisefromthelimitedavailabilityoftraining
data, strict type checking in strongly typed languages, and differences in the APIs and terminologies used.
This study explores the possibilities of LLMs to automate the creation of unit tests for game development.
We start with Code Llama [21] as a base model and refine it using a dataset drawn from game development
examples.
Our contribution can be summarized as follows:
• To the best of our knowledge, this study represents the first investigation of the use of LLMs to
create unit tests specifically for game development, independent of the game engine, considering two
commonly used statically typed languages: C# and C++.
• We present an automatic method for creating corpora based on LLMs to generate synthetic datasets
of unit tests for training models or evaluation purposes. The goal is to remove the bottleneck of not
having enough data available or the cost of acquiring data by human experts.
• The objectives of the work are defined after discussions with the local game development industry.
The experiments are conducted with two publicly available game engines, Unity1 and Unreal2, which
are used by companies or have the most in common with private engines in companies.
• Our work includes the adaptation of LLM-related processes, such as fine-tuning, prompting, and
retrieval augmented generation (RAG) [15], to typical game development scenarios.
• Our prototype is available on demand at https://github.com/unibuc-cs/

5.2. Quantitative Evaluation of generated tests
For each of the 590 MUTs in the source code dataset, we instruct the model to generate 5 unit tests,
resulting in a total number of 2950 unit tests produced. In the experiments, we keep the same number of
generated tests and average the results obtained over 10 independent samples.
